brass-ink.note: Readable dark-gold for text/labels; aged-brass is too light to read as text on cream.

warm-limestone.note: Shared ink-to-paper neutral spine: deep-pine-ink and pine-ink-soft are the dark end, oat-surface/warm-limestone the light end.

stone-muted.note: Tertiary text + micro-labels only; ~3.7:1 on cream, below AA for body text.

sourceKey.note: Per-data-source color families — a key, not theme accents (The Source-Color Rule).

display.purpose: Fluid clamp() numerals — OPE exam id + section accuracy only. Space Grotesk, tabular-nums.

headline.purpose: Primary section titles. Manrope 700, tight tracking.

body.purpose: All running UI text + prose. Space Grotesk 400, lh 1.6.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked captions/eyebrows/table-headers/chips. The most repeated type move.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Token --shadow. Cards, top bar, sticky nav. Barely-there resting lift.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Token --shadow-2. The modal, above a rgba(12,16,14,0.48) scrim.

shadows.name: btn-primary
shadows.purpose: Green-tinted micro-lift on the primary button (hover: 0 1px 4px rgba(61,122,94,0.22)).

shadows.name: coach-fab
shadows.purpose: Floating AI coach FAB green glow (hover deepens to 0 4px 16px rgba(61,122,94,0.35)).

shadows.name: coach-panel
shadows.purpose: Floating chat dock — the most pronounced elevation in the app.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Token --ease. Fast-out, gentle-settle curve for all transitions.

motion.name: rise
motion.value: @keyframes rise { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(6px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid sage-green CTA with a green micro-lift shadow and a gold focus ring.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; background: #3d7a5e; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(61,122,94,0.15); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1), box-shadow 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #35705a; box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(61,122,94,0.22); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(196,168,67,0.7); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Ghost-bordered green action for secondary tasks beside a primary CTA.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(61,122,94,0.5); border-radius: 10px; background: transparent; color: #3d7a5e; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: rgba(61,122,94,0.08); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(196,168,67,0.7); }

components.name: Source Chip
components.description: Cross-table data-source identity marker — one fixed hue family per source, outside the brand tokens.
components.html: Official Guide GMAT Club
components.css: .ds-source-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 22px; padding: 2px 9px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-source-starttest { background: rgba(56,102,209,0.12); color: #1e3a8a; border: 1px solid rgba(56,102,209,0.32); } .ds-source-gmatclub { background: rgba(217,119,6,0.14); color: #92400e; border: 1px solid rgba(217,119,6,0.38); }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Performance-classification taxonomy: STRONG (green) / IMPROVING (gold) / NEEDS-FOCUS (red), soft tinted fills.
components.html: Strong Improving Needs focus
components.css: .ds-status-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 22px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-status-strong { background: rgba(61,122,94,0.10); color: #3d7a5e; border: 1px solid rgba(61,122,94,0.25); } .ds-status-improving { background: rgba(196,168,67,0.10); color: #8f7c35; border: 1px solid rgba(196,168,67,0.25); } .ds-status-needs-focus { background: rgba(181,74,68,0.10); color: #b54a44; border: 1px solid rgba(181,74,68,0.25); }

components.name: Difficulty Chip
components.description: Hard/Medium/Easy difficulty band with an optional inner IRT theta value.
components.css: .ds-difficulty-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.68); font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-difficulty-chip em { font-style: normal; font-size: 0.62rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #7a807c; } .ds-diff-hard { color: #b54a44; border: 1px solid rgba(181,74,68,0.22); background: rgba(181,74,68,0.06); } .ds-diff-medium { color: #8f7c35; border: 1px solid rgba(196,168,67,0.22); background: rgba(196,168,67,0.06); } .ds-diff-easy { color: #3d7a5e; border: 1px solid rgba(61,122,94,0.18); background: rgba(61,122,94,0.05); }

components.name: Answer-Choice Card (signature)
components.description: The centerpiece. Three-color semantic via a 3px left-border + matching tint: gold = correct answer the user missed, red = user's wrong pick, green = user's right pick.
components.html:
AThe right answer you didn't pick
CYour pick — wrong
BYour pick — right
components.css: .ds-choices { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; } .ds-choice { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 12px; padding: 16px; border: 1px solid #dcd8d0; border-left-width: 3px; border-radius: 8px; background: #fdfcf8; color: #2a302c; font-size: 0.9rem; } .ds-choice strong { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 700; } .ds-choice-correct-only { border-left-color: #c4a843; background: rgba(196,168,67,0.06); } .ds-choice-mine-wrong { border-left-color: #b54a44; background: rgba(181,74,68,0.05); } .ds-choice-mine-correct { border-left-color: #3d7a5e; background: rgba(61,122,94,0.06); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Warm off-white surface on the cream page, 1px border, soft resting shadow. Never nest cards.
components.html:
Quant accuracy

You're up 6 points over the last 10 sessions. Number Properties is still the soft spot.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid #dcd8d0; border-radius: 14px; background: #fdfcf8; color: #2a302c; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.03), 0 3px 10px rgba(21,34,27,0.04); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #4a524e; }

components.name: Input
components.description: Sits on the page background so it reads as a well recessed into a card. Green :focus ring, gold :focus-visible outline.
components.css: .ds-input { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 40px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #dcd8d0; border-radius: 10px; background: #f5f4ef; color: #2a302c; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1), box-shadow 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #7a807c; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: #c8c3ba; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #3d7a5e; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(47,109,79,0.14); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c4a843; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Callout
components.description: Non-blocking system message bar; slate-info left edge + info tint, .error variant flips to brick-danger. The second sanctioned thick-left-border.
components.css: .ds-status { padding: 10px 14px; border: 1px solid rgba(74,127,165,0.2); border-left: 3px solid #4a7fa5; border-radius: 0 5px 5px 0; background: rgba(74,127,165,0.08); color: #2a302c; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-status.ds-status-error { border-color: rgba(181,74,68,0.2); border-left-color: #b54a44; background: rgba(181,74,68,0.08); color: #b54a44; }

narrative.overview: The interface of a coach who has read your entire practice record and tells you the truth, kindly — warm without being soft, honest without being harsh, calm under exam pressure. It reads like good notebook paper under a warm desk lamp: forest green carries the work you got right and the system's own voice, brass gold marks the answer you reached for and missed, all on warm off-whites that never glare. Motivation is earned — a shrinking error count and a climbing accuracy bar are the reward, not confetti. Density is two-speed: reading surfaces breathe (15px body, lh 1.6, 16-24px padding) while analytics surfaces are compact and information-dense (8-12px cells, 22px pills, 34px filters) because the data is the hero and the chrome disappears. Depth is near-flat: 1px warm borders and three tonal off-white steps do the structural work; shadow is dialed to a whisper, reserved for things that literally float.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-paper light theme: forest-sage green primary + aged-brass gold accent on cream off-whites.
Flat-by-default depth — borders and tonal surface steps, not shadows.
A relentless uppercase micro-label idiom (0.7rem, tracked, weight 600-700) for every caption, eyebrow, table header, and chip.
A load-bearing three-color answer semantic: gold = the right answer you missed, red = your wrong pick, green = your right pick.
All numerics set tabular-nums so columns and metrics align.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Green and gold are not interchangeable accents. Green speaks for the user's success and the system's own voice; gold speaks for attention — the thing to look at, the answer missed. Never swap them for visual variety.

rules.name: The Earned-Saturation Rule
rules.body: Saturated color appears only where it carries meaning — a metric, a status, a correctness flag, a data source. Decorative color washes are forbidden; warmth comes from the cream surfaces and the type, not from painting panels.

rules.name: The Source-Color Rule
rules.body: Each data source owns one fixed hue family outside the brand tokens, used only on its pill and dot: StartTest indigo, GMAT Club amber, TTP emerald, OPE purple, LSAT rose. A color key, not theme accents — never reuse a source hue for unrelated UI.

rules.name: The Micro-Label Rule
rules.body: Captions, eyebrows, table headers, and chip text are always the same atom: uppercase, ~0.7rem, weight 600-700, letter-spacing ~0.07em. One idiom, reused everywhere. Never set a caption as sentence-case lowercase gray.

rules.name: The Two-Family Rule
rules.body: Manrope is for titles and only titles (plus the editorial OPE overlines). Everything else is Space Grotesk. No third family; no body in Manrope, no titles in Space Grotesk.

rules.name: The Tabular-Number Rule
rules.body: Every metric, accuracy figure, count, time, and theta uses font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Numbers are data; they must align in columns and never jitter as they update.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. To separate two elements, try a tonal surface step or a 1px border first. A shadow is permitted only when the element genuinely floats above the page (modal, dropdown, FAB, drag preview).

rules.name: The Green-Ink Shadow Rule
rules.body: Diffuse shadow layers use rgba(21,34,27,...), never pure black. Pure-black shadows on this cream surface read cold and cheap.

dos: Do keep saturated color rationed — green for the user's success and the system's voice, gold for attention/the missed answer, per The Two-Voice Rule.
Do build depth from 1px warm-border + the three tonal off-white steps first; add a shadow only for things that float (modal, dropdown, FAB).
Do set every caption, eyebrow, table header, and chip as the uppercase ~0.7rem tracked micro-label, and every number as tabular-nums.
Do keep Manrope for titles and Space Grotesk for everything else — two families, no more.
Do preserve the gold/red/green answer semantic exactly (gold = correct-missed, red = wrong-pick, green = right-pick) wherever correctness is shown.
Do use pine-ink-soft (#4a524e) for any text a reader must actually read; reserve stone-muted for bold uppercase micro-labels.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion for new motion — the main dashboard's rise/pulse/coach animations are currently not covered by a global guard; close that gap rather than widen it.
donts: Don't gamify. No mascots, no confetti-on-streak, no XP bars, no toy-bright cartoon energy. Progress is shown as honest data, never a slot-machine reward.
Don't drift toward enterprise BI. No dense chrome, no nested config panels, no toolbar thickets, no charts that bury the one insight under ten controls.
Don't set body or placeholder text in stone-muted (#7a807c) on the cream surfaces — it lands near 3.7:1, under the 4.5:1 floor. Bump to pine-ink-soft for anything readers read.
Don't add border-left/right greater than 1px as a decorative colored stripe. The only legal thick left-border is the established answer/status semantic (gold/red/green/info).
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, the big-number hero-metric template, or identical icon+heading+text card grids.
Don't spread the Study Plan's indigo/violet, inline-styled palette into the main dashboard — it's a known inconsistency; bring new study-plan work back toward the sage/gold token system.
Don't reuse a data-source hue (indigo/amber/emerald/purple/rose) for unrelated UI — those colors are a source key, not theme accents.
